thesis: Gyrodyne, LLC is liquidating two remaining New York real estate properties (Flowerfield on Long Island and Cortlandt Manor in Westchester) under ASC 205-30 liquidation-basis accounting. **[Fact]** As of March 31, 2026, net assets in liquidation were **$25,924,002** (**$11.79** per share on **2,199,308** shares) versus a thin-market quote near **$5.80**. **[Fact]** A signed B2K agreement covers the largest Flowerfield parcel for **$24.0M–$28.74M**, contingent on subdivision and site-plan approval. **[Fact]** Target dissolution is **December 31, 2028**, twice extended from an original 2026 target.
why_market_wrong: The company publishes an audited liquidation NAV and a dated payoff path, but micro-cap illiquidity (~2,000 shares/day), an unresolved Article 78 appeal on Flowerfield, and K-1-style obscurity keep most investors away. **[Inference]** The discount to disclosed NAV looks like a market-structure and litigation-timing mispricing rather than a hidden operating failure. **[Inference]** Proof-first component math at base case sums to **$11.79/sh** economic value versus price near **$5.80**.

key_assumptions.input: Disclosed net assets in liquidation per share

key_assumptions.source: Five filing-backed net_asset_value proofs summing to disclosed NAV

open_questions: B2K Smithtown site-plan approval timing (company expected Q3 2026).
Human sign-off before sizing up despite mechanical accumulate signal.
